Thanks for opening the discussion. Based on the latest market reference I’m seeing, I can offer supply at 6.50 per unit, which reflects current pricing and gives us a clean starting point for a committed volume conversation. If you’re looking at a meaningful order size or a structured commitment, I’m open to discussing how that could affect terms. This is the opening turn with no buyer offer to respond to, so making an anchored opening offer is the best move. The latest market check shows 6.50, which supports a strong initial ask well above the supplier floor while leaving room to negotiate on volume or terms.

I can’t move forward at 6.50 per unit. That level is far outside the range I can support on a buy and doesn’t leave a workable margin for downstream commitments, so there isn’t enough overlap to justify countering from here. If your pricing changes materially toward a realistic procurement level, I’d be open to reopening the discussion.
